summarization.a: The figure displays X-ray Absorption Spectroscopy (XAS) data in Total Fluorescence Yield (TFY) mode for O 1s transitions for five different films.
summarization.b: The figure displays XAS (TFY) data for Fe 2p transitions for five different films.

data_extraction.a:
| Index | Fe2O3:MgO cycle ratio | Temperature |  Photon energy (eV) |
|---|---|---|---|
| e | 100:10 | 375 | Peaks at 530, 542 |
| d | 100:2 | 375 | Peaks at 530, 542 |
| c | Non-doped | 375 | Peaks at 530, 542 |
| b | 100:1 | 400 | Peaks at 530, 532, 542 |
| a | Non-doped | 400 | Peaks at 530, 532, 542 |

a.question: How does the effect of annealing become apparent from the XAS spectra?
a.answer: When the films are annealed, the hematite could transition to (a larger fraction of) magnetite. Visually, in the Fe 2p region, the smaller L_3 peak at 708.5 eV shifts toward the main L_3 peak at 710 eV in that case. A similar trend is also observable for the O 1s region where the peak at 530 eV shifts toward 532 eV. Annealing has no significant effect on films grown at 400 °C (a, b), but does have a significant effect on films grown at 375 °C (c, d, e).
